channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Amid discord and disagreement, the Antarctic Treaty System is failing | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Antarctic Treaty is glorified as evidence of managing cooperation in a contested world. Forged in the Cold War, the treaty system has effectively held conflict at bay for almost seven decades. Geopolitics has been ...

item.title: 🏴 The Inertia of Russia’s War | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why Putin can’t end the conflict.

item.title: 🌍 Iran’s New Grand Strategy | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How a remade Islamic Republic will reshape the Middle East.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Launches New Attacks On Kuwait, Bahrain (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: U.S. Central Command says it "conducted self-defense strikes on an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island." The post Iran Launches New Attacks On Kuwait, Bahrain (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 A General Assembly that backed international rules would put Philippines on Security Council | ASPI Strategist
item.description: On 3 June in New York, the UN General Assembly faces a choice: will the institution charged with enforcing international rules include a voice that’s prepared to call out their breaches? The General Assembly will ...

item.title: 🕵️ Any Plans For Direct U-28 Draco Special Ops Surveillance Plane Replacement Abandoned | The War Zone
item.description: There are signs now that the U.S. special operations community is on track to stop flying dedicated crewed surveillance planes entirely. The post Any Plans For Direct U-28 Draco Special Ops Surveillance Plane Replacement Abandoned appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The US military wants to showcase battle-ready laser weapons by 2028 | Defense News
item.description: A directed energy demonstration is expected to occur during the summer of 2028, as part of a series of planned Golden Dome-related events.

item.title: 🏛️ What Congress Should Do About the President’s Sweetheart Deal in Trump v. IRS | Just Security
item.description: Tax law experts offer three actions that Congress must take to fully unwind the Trump administration’s settlement and hold its architects accountable. The post What Congress Should Do About the President’s Sweetheart Deal in Trump v. IRS appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌏 The Deadly Price of China’s Coal Boom | Foreign Policy
item.description: Two fatal disasters highlight enduring problems in the mining industry.

item.title: 🕵️ How AI is shaping the future of geospatial intelligence | Breaking Defense
item.description: From AI adoption and explainability to new commercial satellite contracts, Breaking Defense’s latest eBook brings together reporting from GEOINT 2026 on how intelligence leaders are shaping the future of space-based geospatial intelligence.

item.title: 🕵️ Trump executive order on AI gives central role to NSA | Breaking Defense
item.description: The EO creates a “voluntary framework” for AI developers to give the government early access to their latest tech.

item.title: 🏴 Russia Pounds Ukraine With Missile, Drone Strikes | Foreign Policy
item.description: Moscow warned of a massive attack on Kyiv. And it delivered.

item.title: 🏴 As Russia’s Ukraine invasion unravels, Putin escalates attacks on civilians | Atlantic Council
item.description: Russia launched a major bombardment of Ukrainian cities on June 2 as Vladimir Putin continues to escalate attacks on Ukraine's civilian population amid growing signs that his invasion is unraveling, writes Peter Dickinson. The post As Russia’s Ukraine invasion unravels, Putin…

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine is now Europe’s shield but still needs more help to stop Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: Europeans increasingly recognize Ukraine as the continent's shield against Russian aggression, but this must not fuel complacency over the country's ability to bear the current burden indefinitely, writes Olena Tregub. The post Ukraine is now Europe’s shield but still needs more…

item.title: 🏴 Two Senators Just Blew Up Trump’s Boat-Strike Justifications | Foreign Policy
item.description: Sens. Kaine and Paul say that the military’s targeting criteria don’t include the presence of drugs or arms.

item.title: 🌍 Predictability as power: Australia’s advantage in the AI infrastructure race | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Remoteness from conflict is emerging as a key advantage for Australia in the competition to host AI data centres. The country already enjoys great advantages including prospectively abundant and cheap energy from renewable sources. Israeli ...

item.title: 🌏 The Gulf Crisis Is Forcing Japan Into an Energy Rethink | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tokyo spent decades preparing for disruption—then found its options narrowing.

item.title: 🔐 AI makes it easy to expose holes in Army’s unified network, official says | Breaking Defense
item.description: New AI capabilities are “lowering the barrier of entry” to exploit vulnerabilities, according to the Army’s chief data officer.

item.title: 🌏 How will the Iran war change the US role in the world? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts weigh in on whether the United States should “pivot” its attention away from the Middle East and toward the Indo-Pacific. The post How will the Iran war change the US role in the world? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🔐 How defensive cyber responds to hockey-stick growth of AI-driven threats | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Artificial intelligence is reshaping adversarial cyber operations at machine speed, requiring defenders to increase operational tempo while imposing pain and cost.

item.title: 📍 New bill aims to regulate military uses of AI | Defense One
item.description: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand also wants to establish a clear chain of human accountability for AI on the battlefield.

item.title: 📍 Five charts that show Latin America’s search for economic growth starts in health systems | Atlantic Council
item.description: Health investments could boost economic productivity significantly—if the countries direct their expenditures toward efforts that tangibly improve public health. The post Five charts that show Latin America’s search for economic growth starts in health systems appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 Does the Quad Still Matter? | War on the Rocks
item.description: On May 26, India hosted a formal meeting of the foreign ministers of the Quad — comprising the United States, Australia, India, and Japan. Since its initial creation in 2007 and revival in 2017, foreign policy analysts have debated the usefulness of the organization, which was…

item.title: 🏴 Rheinmetall locks in wide-ranging $6.6 billion weapons package with Romania | Breaking Defense
item.description: Romania is due to become the second Lynx operator on NATO’s eastern flank after Hungary’s 2020 order of 218 vehicles.

item.title: 📍 Future presence of US troops in Lithuania is ‘under review,’ says minister | Defense News
item.description: U.S. troops presently stationed in the country are now leaving as expected, but the next scheduled group is not arriving, the defense minister said.

item.title: 📍 Are Ukrainian Mirage 2000s Now Flying Air-To-Ground Strike Missions? | The War Zone
item.description: A low-level run followed by a steep climb hints at a ground-attack mission as part of the Mirage’s expanding combat repertoire. The post Are Ukrainian Mirage 2000s Now Flying Air-To-Ground Strike Missions? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Trump signs business-friendlier version of AI executive order | Defense One
item.description: The order asks for just a month of pre-publication access to advanced models, not the 90 days of an earlier draft.

item.title: 🏛️ Next Generation Penetrator Bomb Slated To Replace MOP Has Been Designated GBU-76 | The War Zone
item.description: The official designation comes as the Air Force plans to field the GBU-76 take shape. The post Next Generation Penetrator Bomb Slated To Replace MOP Has Been Designated GBU-76 appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Braw in Economist Enterprise on the benefits of decreased globalization | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 2, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw mentioned in an article in the Economist Enterprise on how the end of globalization could bring unexpected benefits. The post Braw in Economist Enterprise on the benefits of decreased globalization…

item.title: 🏴 Braw in The Guardian on the importance of citizen resilience | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 2, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw published an article in The Guardian discussing the importance of civilian preparedness and how NATO allies can find inspiration in the Scandinavian and Baltic regions. The post Braw in The Guardian on the…
